expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves Google Sheets export entry points, scope boundaries, and format constraints before scenario drafting
expectations.evidence:
In result.md, the Phase 1 snapshot is structured explicitly around the three required context elements and keeps them in intake form (not scenarios):
- "## 2.1 Google Sheets export entry points (to be preserved)" → "Unknown"
- "## 2.2 Scope boundaries (in-scope / out-of-scope)" → "Unknown"
- "## 2.3 Format constraints (Google Sheets export)" → "Unknown"
It also directly states the case-focus expectation under "### Case focus coverage check" and explains it is "NOT DEMONSTRABLE" due to missing admissible evidence, which still constitutes explicit coverage of the required items in the intake.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: result.md is titled "# Phase 1 — Context Intake (Contract Check)" and stays in context intake/constraints mode, explicitly avoiding scenario drafting. It includes "### Phase alignment check" with "Status: PASS — this output avoids scenarios and stays in intake/constraints mode, while clearly marking unknowns due to evidence unavailability."
